reforms.description: A major tax reform is set to take effect in early 1983, aimed at reducing marginal tax rates and modifying the deductibility of interest payments, which is expected to alleviate adverse effects on wage formation and incentives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing marginal tax rates, the reform is expected to enhance incentives for work and investment, potentially leading to increased economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The major tax reform will take effect in early 1983.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the tax system, aimed at improving economic incentives and addressing long-standing issues related to high marginal tax rates.
reforms.source_quote: A major tax reform, receiving broad Parliamentary support, will take effect in early 1983.
